body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44894", "cvss": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-940", "name": "Improper Verification of Source of a Communication Channel"}], "description": "NoQuicTokenHandler is the tokenHandler used when the application does not set one. Its writeToken() returns false (server will not send Retry — acceptable), but validateToken() unconditionally `return 0`. In QuicheQuicServerCodec.handlePacket(), a non-negative return from validateToken() is interpreted as 'token is valid, ODCID starts at offset 0', causing the server to call quiche_accept as if the client's address had been validated by a Retry round-trip. Per RFC 9000 §8.1, a validated address lifts the 3× anti-amplification send limit. Thus any attacker who includes ANY non-empty token bytes in an Initial packet — with a spoofed victim source IP — causes the Netty server to treat the victim as validated and reflect full-size handshake flights (certificates, etc.) toward it without the 3× cap. The correct 'no token handler' semantics would be to return -1 (invalid) so the normal un-validated path and amplification limit apply.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00143, "percentile": 0.03938}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-cmm3-54f8-px4j", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-08T22:59:43Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-cmm3-54f8-px4j", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-cmm3-54f8-px4j"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44894"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-12T15:16:26Z", "published_at": "2026-06-08T22:59:43Z", "references": ["https://github.com/netty/netty/security/advisories/GHSA-cmm3-54f8-px4j", "https://github.com/netty/netty/releases/tag/netty-4.2.15.Final", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44894", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-cmm3-54f8-px4j"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/netty/netty/security-advisories/GHSA-cmm3-54f8-px4j", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/netty/netty", "summary": "Netty's Default QUIC token handler accepts any client-supplied token", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-12T19:29:18Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-cmm3-54f8-px4j", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "4.2.15.Final", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "io.netty:netty-codec-classes-quic"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 4.2.0.Final, <= 4.2.14.Final"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Netty's Default QUIC token handler accepts any client-supplied token
